items.title: Define memory entry schema and writeMemoryEntry helper in packages/core/src/memory/types.ts
items.description: This is the foundational contract for all cross-cycle learning. Three downstream items (review-phase wiring, API backend migration, integration test) cannot be implemented without the schema and file-lock helper it exports. Low cost relative to the number of items it unblocks.

items.title: Fix the /org dashboard route — verify org graph endpoint returns real delegation data and page renders nodes/edges
items.description: A P1 fix on a currently broken page that surfaces the delegation graph the autonomous loop depends on for operator oversight. Touching org-graph.ts and the Svelte page is a contained two-file fix with high visibility payoff. No dependencies on other sprint items.

items.title: Fix the /agents dashboard route — verify agent listing shows real agents from .agentforge/agents/*.yaml
items.description: Another broken P1 page that prevents operators from confirming which agents are active in the current team. A quick frontend verification and wiring fix with no required backend changes; high confidence and low cost.

items.title: Wire the review phase handler to write memory entries of type=review-finding for MAJOR/CRITICAL findings
items.description: Seeds the cross-cycle learning flywheel — without capturing review findings as typed JSONL entries, every cycle restarts with zero institutional knowledge. Requires the schema from rank-1 but delivers the single most important learning signal in the system.

items.title: Fix /api/v5/memory backend to read new .agentforge/memory/*.jsonl files with filtering and sort
items.description: Upgrades the memory API from static JSON/md to the new JSONL format defined in rank-1, unblocking both the memory dashboard UI (rank-9) and the flywheel stats card (rank-16). A mid-chain enabler with two downstream leaf consumers at low incremental cost.

items.title: Wire /approvals dashboard page to GET/POST /api/v5/cycles/:id/approval endpoints with SSE modal
items.description: The server-side approval endpoints already shipped in v6.7.4; this item only wires the dashboard UI and the SSE-triggered global ApprovalModal. Human budget approval is a core governance gate of the autonomous loop — a missing UI here blocks operator oversight entirely.

items.title: Complete the /runner dashboard route — fix MODEL_TIERS errors, wire agent dispatch, add real-time streaming output
items.description: MODEL_TIERS undefined errors are compile-time failures surfaced by svelte-check, meaning this page is actively broken. Fixing the type import, wiring the dispatch form, and adding streaming output restores operator ability to manually trigger agents mid-cycle.

items.title: Render markdown content in cycle phases tab using marked or markdown-it
items.description: A small-library, high-polish change that transforms opaque raw JSON blobs into readable reviewer responses and gate rationales. Significantly improves operator comprehension of cycle outcomes at very low cost and zero backend risk.

items.title: Update /memory dashboard page to render live JSONL feed with type chips, source links, search, and filter
items.description: Replaces the static memory page with a live feed driven by the new JSONL API (rank-5). Type chips, clickable source links to cycles/agents, and expandable JSON entries give operators the primary window into what the system has learned. Supersedes rank-12 if rank-5 is complete.

items.title: Wire /flywheel dashboard page to real metrics computed from cycles, sprints, and sessions
items.description: Static gauges on the flywheel page provide zero signal about loop health. Computing real meta-learning rate, autonomy score, capability inheritance, and velocity from live data gives operators the primary health KPI for the autonomous loop's self-improvement trajectory.

items.title: Fix /search dashboard route — implement or wire backend search across sessions, cycles, agents, sprints, memories
items.description: Search currently returns zero results regardless of query — a completely broken operator workflow. Wiring it to existing data endpoints (sessions, cycles, agents, sprints, memories) closes a significant navigation gap without requiring new persistence infrastructure.

items.title: Wire /memory dashboard page to read .agentforge/data/memories.json — add search and agent filter (basic fallback)
items.description: Provides a simpler initial memory-page wiring against the legacy memories.json before the full JSONL migration from rank-5 and rank-9 lands. Lower priority because rank-9 supersedes it, but useful as a safe fallback if the JSONL pipeline is delayed in execution.

items.title: Complete /branches dashboard tab — list autonomous/* branches with cycle, age, PR status, and stale-delete button
items.description: Operational hygiene feature giving operators visibility into the autonomous branch lifecycle, stale-branch detection, and deletion. Reduces git noise between cycles and provides a cleanup affordance that is otherwise missing from the dashboard.

items.title: Verify and complete /sprints/[version] detail page — title, status, kanban, audit findings, completion percentage
items.description: Described as mostly built — a verification and gap-fill pass rather than a full build. Low effort with high closure value on a page operators use to review every sprint's deliverables. Low risk of regressions.

items.title: Verify /live activity feed renders cycle_event SSE messages with correct colors, types, and timestamps
items.description: A quick smoke-check that confirms the SSE subscription wiring is correct and events render properly. Validates a critical realtime observability path at minimal cost; high confidence given the narrow scope.

items.title: Add memory stats card to /flywheel — total entries, entries-per-cycle trend, memory hit rate
items.description: Adds quantitative memory-system health signals (entry count, trend, hit rate) to the flywheel page. Depends on both the JSONL API (rank-5) and the real flywheel metrics (rank-10); sequencing after both avoids rework. Meaningful but not blocking.

items.title: Add tests/integration/memory-flow.test.ts — full two-cycle run asserting memory write, persist, and audit reuse
items.description: End-to-end validation that the entire memory pipeline — write, persist, retrieve, reuse — works across two consecutive cycles. High value as a regression gate but requires the schema (rank-1), review wiring (rank-4), and API backend (rank-5) to all be stable first.

items.title: Add Playwright e2e tests under tests/e2e/ covering all major dashboard routes with real data
items.description: The broadest test-coverage addition in the sprint, exercising all major dashboard routes end-to-end. Highest cost item and widest dependency set — must run after the page fixes in ranks 2, 3, 6, 14, and 15 are stable to avoid flaky tests against broken pages.
